---
title: "Microeconometrics Using Stata"
Authors:
- Colin Cameron
- Pravin Trivedi
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 5
    toc-expand: true
    toc-title: Contents
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
execute:
  enabled: true
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

[Microeconometrics with R](https://ycroissant.github.io/micsr_book/)

[UCLA](https://stats.oarc.ucla.edu/stata/modules/)

[Github](https://github.com/thousandoaks?tab=repositories)

# Julia setup


```{julia}
using StatEcon
const DATA = let c = filter(d -> isdir(d) && (isfile(joinpath(d, "auto.dta")) || isdir(joinpath(d, "musr"))), ["../../data", "../data", "data"]); isempty(c) ? error("StatEcon example data not found; run this notebook from its own folder (examples/Cameron_Trivedi) or the repo root.") : first(c) end
using DataFrames, ReadStatTables, CSV
using Statistics, StatsBase, LinearAlgebra, Printf, Random
using FixedEffectModels, StatsModels, GLM
using FixedEffectModels: Vcov
using CairoMakie
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "png")
using Optim
import Distributions
using Distributions: cdf, pdf, quantile, Normal, TDist, FDist, Chisq

# -- ReadStatTables labelled-value unwrap (mirrors StatEcon internals)
_rawval(x) = hasproperty(x, :value) ? x.value : x

# -- Pedagogical helper: quantile regression by direct check-loss minimization
# with Optim + NelderMead. Used in the by-hand demonstrations throughout the
# chapter. For the polished Stata-style workflow use `stata_qreg` / `stata_sqreg`
# / `stata_qcount` from StatEcon.
function quantreg(X, y, τ)
    n, p = size(X)
    function loss(β)
        r = y .- X * β
        sum(@. ifelse(r >= 0, τ * r, (τ - 1) * r))
    end
    β0 = (X' * X) \ (X' * y)                           # OLS warm start
    res = Optim.optimize(loss, β0, Optim.NelderMead(),
                         Optim.Options(iterations = 5000, x_abstol = 1e-8))
    return Optim.minimizer(res)
end
```


# 7 Quantile regression

## 7.1 Introduction

## 7.2 QR

### 7.2.1 Conditional quantiles

### 7.2.2 Computation of QR estimates and standard errors

### 7.2.3 The qreg, bsqreg, and sqreg commands

## 7.3 QR for medical expenditures data

Quantile regression in Julia: there is no single canonical package; we implement the check-loss minimization manually with `Optim.jl`.


### 7.3.1 Data summary

```{julia}
#=
clear all
set scheme s1mono  /* Graphics scheme */

********** 7.3: QUANTILE REGRESSION FOR MEDICAL EXPENDITURES DATA

* Read in log of medical expenditures data and summarize
use musr/mus03data.dta, clear
drop if ltotexp == . 
summarize ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, separator(0)
=#
# Read log medical expenditures data
df7 = DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus03data.dta"))
df7 = dropmissing(df7, :ltotexp)
DataFrames.describe(df7[:, [:ltotexp, :suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white]])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Quantile plot for ltotexp (qplot replacement)
* Create percentiles and plot
preserve
sort ltotexp
generate pctile = _n / _N
twoway line ltotexp pctile, ///
    ytitle("quantiles of ln(totexp) if totexp>0") xtitle("Fraction of the data") ///
    scale(1.5) name(qplot_ltotexp, replace)
quietly graph export musr/mus07fig1_qltotexp.eps, replace 
restore
=#
stata_summarize(df7, [:ltotexp, :suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white])
```

```{julia}
# Quantile plot for ltotexp
fig7 = Figure(size = (700, 400))
ax = Axis(fig7[1, 1], xlabel = "Fraction of data",
          ylabel = "quantiles of ln(totexp) | totexp > 0")
sorted_y = sort(df7.ltotexp)
pct = (1:length(sorted_y)) ./ length(sorted_y)
lines!(ax, pct, sorted_y)
fig7
```

### 7.3.2 QR estimates

```{julia}
#=
* Basic quantile regression for q = 0.5
qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white
=#
# Median (q = 0.5) regression
df7c = dropmissing(df7[:, [:ltotexp, :suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white]])
y    = df7c.ltotexp
X    = hcat(ones(nrow(df7c)),
            df7c.suppins, df7c.totchr, df7c.age, df7c.female, df7c.white)
βqr  = quantreg(X, y, 0.5)
xnms = ["_cons", "suppins", "totchr", "age", "female", "white"]
println("\n— Quantile regression at τ = 0.5 —")
for i in eachindex(βqr)
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", xnms[i], βqr[i])
end
```

```{julia}
#* Basic quantile regression for q = 0.5
#qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white
stata_qreg(df7, :ltotexp,
           [:suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white]; τ = 0.5);
```

### 7.3.3 Interpretation of conditional quantile coefficients

### 7.3.4 Retransformation

```{julia}
#=
* Obtain multiplier to convert QR coeffs in logs to AME in levels. 
quietly predict xb
generate expxb = exp(xb)
quietly summarize expxb
display "Multiplier of QR in logs coeffs to get AME in levels = " r(mean)
=#
# Multiplier exp(x'β) — converts QR coefficients in logs to AME in levels
xb     = X * βqr
expxb  = exp.(xb)
@printf("Mean of exp(x'β) = %.4f (multiplier)\n", mean(expxb))
```

### 7.3.5 Comparison of estimates at different quantiles

```{julia}
#=
* Compare (1) OLS; (2-4) coeffs across quantiles; (5) bootstrap SEs
quietly regress ltotexp suppins totchr age female white   
estimates store OLS
quietly qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quantile(.25)  
estimates store QR_25
quietly qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quantile(.50) 
estimates store QR_50
quietly qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quantile(.75) 
estimates store QR_75
set seed 10101 
quietly bsqreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quant(.50) reps(400) 
estimates store BSQR_50 
estimates table OLS QR_25 QR_50 QR_75 BSQR_50, b(%7.3f) se 
=#
# OLS + QR at τ = 0.25, 0.50, 0.75
ols7  = (X' * X) \ (X' * y)
qr25  = quantreg(X, y, 0.25)
qr50  = quantreg(X, y, 0.50)
qr75  = quantreg(X, y, 0.75)

println("\n— OLS vs Quantile Regressions —")
@printf("%-10s %10s %10s %10s %10s\n",
        "Variable", "OLS", "QR_25", "QR_50", "QR_75")
for i in eachindex(ols7)
    @printf("%-10s %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f\n",
            xnms[i], ols7[i], qr25[i], qr50[i], qr75[i])
end
```

```{julia}
# Bootstrap SEs at τ = 0.5  (B = 200)
Random.seed!(10101)
B = 200
β_boot = Matrix{Float64}(undef, B, length(βqr))
for b in 1:B
    idx = sample(1:length(y), length(y); replace = true)
    β_boot[b, :] = quantreg(X[idx, :], y[idx], 0.5)
end
se_boot = vec(std(β_boot, dims = 1))
println("\n— Bootstrap SE for τ = 0.5 —")
for i in eachindex(βqr)
    @printf("%-10s coef=%10.4f  bs.se=%10.4f\n",
            xnms[i], βqr[i], se_boot[i])
end
```

```{julia}
#=
* Compare (1) OLS; (2-4) coeffs across quantiles; (5) bootstrap SEs
quietly regress ltotexp suppins totchr age female white
estimates store OLS
quietly qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quantile(.25)
estimates store QR_25
quietly qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quantile(.50)
estimates store QR_50
quietly qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quantile(.75)
estimates store QR_75
set seed 10101
quietly bsqreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quant(.50) reps(400)
estimates store BSQR_50
estimates table OLS QR_25 QR_50 QR_75 BSQR_50, b(%7.3f) se
=#
_wrap_fem(m) = (;
    β         = StatsBase.coef(m),
    V         = StatsBase.vcov(m),
    se        = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(StatsBase.vcov(m)), 0.0)),
    coefnames = replace.(string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m)), "(Intercept)" => "_cons"),
    n         = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m)),
)

xqr = [:suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white]

OLS   = _wrap_fem(FixedEffectModels.reg(df7,
            @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + totchr + age + female + white)))
QR_25 = stata_qreg(df7, :ltotexp, xqr; τ=0.25, quiet=true)
QR_50 = stata_qreg(df7, :ltotexp, xqr; τ=0.50, quiet=true)
QR_75 = stata_qreg(df7, :ltotexp, xqr; τ=0.75, quiet=true)

# Bootstrap SEs for τ = 0.5 (re-fit qreg on 400 paired resamples).
Random.seed!(10101)
B      = 400
nb     = DataFrames.nrow(df7)
k_q    = length(QR_50.β)
β_boot = Matrix{Float64}(undef, B, k_q)
for b in 1:B
    idx = StatsBase.sample(1:nb, nb; replace=true)
    fit_b = stata_qreg(df7[idx, :], :ltotexp, xqr; τ=0.5, quiet=true)
    β_boot[b, :] = fit_b.β
end
BSQR_50 = (; β         = QR_50.β,
             V         = zeros(k_q, k_q),
             se        = vec(Statistics.std(β_boot; dims=1)),
             coefnames = QR_50.coefnames,
             n         = QR_50.n)

stata_estimates_table(
    [OLS, QR_25, QR_50, QR_75, BSQR_50],
    ["OLS", "QR_25", "QR_50", "QR_75", "BSQR_50"];
    vars_order = ["suppins", "totchr", "age", "female", "white", "_cons"],
    fmt        = "%7.3f")
```

### 7.3.6 Heteroskedasticity test

```{julia}
# Breusch–Pagan in linear model
m_ols7 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df7c, @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + totchr + age + female + white))
yhat7  = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_ols7, df7c)
df7c.uhat2 = (df7c.ltotexp .- yhat7).^2
m_bp7 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df7c, @formula(uhat2 ~ suppins + totchr + age + female + white))
LM = nrow(df7c) * StatsBase.r2(m_bp7)
@printf("BP LM(5) = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", LM, 1 - cdf(Chisq(5), LM))
```

### 7.3.7 Hypothesis tests

```{julia}
#=
* Simultaneous QR regression with several values of q
set seed 10101
sqreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, q(.25 .50 .75) reps(400)
=#
# Simultaneous QR with bootstrap-jointly: estimate at three τ then test β_τ equality
Random.seed!(10101)
β25_b = Matrix{Float64}(undef, B, length(βqr))
β50_b = Matrix{Float64}(undef, B, length(βqr))
β75_b = Matrix{Float64}(undef, B, length(βqr))
for b in 1:B
    idx = sample(1:length(y), length(y); replace = true)
    β25_b[b, :] = quantreg(X[idx, :], y[idx], 0.25)
    β50_b[b, :] = quantreg(X[idx, :], y[idx], 0.50)
    β75_b[b, :] = quantreg(X[idx, :], y[idx], 0.75)
end
i_supp = findfirst(==("suppins"), xnms)
diff = β25_b[:, i_supp] .- β75_b[:, i_supp]
@printf("β25(suppins) - β75(suppins): mean=%.4f  bs.se=%.4f  z=%.2f\n",
        mean(diff), std(diff), mean(diff) / std(diff))
```


```{julia}
#=
* Test of coefficient equality across QR with different q
test [q25=q50=q75]: suppins
=#
#* Simultaneous QR regression with several values of q
#set seed 10101
#sqreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, q(.25 .50 .75) reps(400)
sq_fit = stata_sqreg(df7, :ltotexp,
                     [:suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white];
                     τs=[0.25, 0.50, 0.75], reps=400, seed=10101);
```

```{julia}
#* Test of coefficient equality across QR with different q
#test [q25=q50=q75]: suppins
stata_test_sureg(sq_fit,
           ["[q25]suppins = [q50]suppins",
            "[q25]suppins = [q75]suppins"];
           df_F = sq_fit.df_resid);
```

### 7.3.8 Graphical display of coefficients over quantiles

```{julia}
#=
* Plots of each regressor's coefficients as quantile q varies
*ssc install grqreg
quietly bsqreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quantile(.50) reps(400)
label variable suppins "=1 if supp ins"
label variable totchr "# of chronic condns"
grqreg, cons ci ols olsci scale(1.1)
quietly graph export musr/mus07fig3_qrcoeff.eps, replace
=#
# grqreg, cons ci ols olsci  — Plot ALL regressors' coefficients as quantile τ varies
τs = collect(0.10:0.05:0.90)
nτ = length(τs)
p = size(X, 2)
βmat   = Matrix{Float64}(undef, nτ, p)   # coefficients at each τ
lo_mat = similar(βmat); up_mat = similar(βmat)
ols_coef = (X'X) \ (X'y)

Random.seed!(10101)
for (j, τ) in enumerate(τs)
    β = quantreg(X, y, τ)
    βmat[j, :] = β
    bs = Matrix{Float64}(undef, 50, p)
    for b in 1:50
        idx = sample(1:length(y), length(y); replace = true)
        bs[b, :] = quantreg(X[idx, :], y[idx], τ)
    end
    se = vec(std(bs, dims = 1))
    lo_mat[j, :] = β .- 1.96 .* se
    up_mat[j, :] = β .+ 1.96 .* se
end

# Panel plot: one subplot per regressor (including constant)
fig_grq = Figure(size = (900, 700))
ncols = 3; nrows = ceil(Int, p / ncols)
for k in 1:p
    r, c = divrem(k - 1, ncols)
    ax = Axis(fig_grq[r+1, c+1], xlabel = "quantile τ", ylabel = xnms[k])
    band!(ax, τs, lo_mat[:, k], up_mat[:, k], color = (:blue, 0.15))
    lines!(ax, τs, βmat[:, k], color = :blue, linewidth = 2)
    hlines!(ax, [ols_coef[k]], color = :red, linestyle = :dash, linewidth = 1)
end
fig_grq
```

## 7.4 QR for generated heteroskedastic data

### 7.4.1 Simulated dataset

```{julia}
#=
clear all
* Generated dataset with heteroskedastic errors
set seed 10101
set obs 10000
generate x2 = rchi2(1)
generate x3 = 5*rnormal(0)
generate e = 5*rnormal(0)
generate u = (.1+0.5*x2)*e
generate y = 1 + 1*x2 + 1*x3 + u
summarize e x2 x3 u y
* Save generated variables to CSV for reuse (Julia, R, etc.)
export delimited y x2 x3 e u using "musr/7_4_1.csv", replace
=#
# Heteroskedastic DGP — load the dataset exported by Stata
# (Stata seed 10101 differs from Julia's RNG, so reading the CSV ensures
#  identical values across both notebooks.)
df7g = CSV.read("$(DATA)/musr/7_4_1.csv", DataFrame)
for c in names(df7g)
    df7g[!, c] = Float64.(df7g[!, c])
end
N7  = DataFrames.nrow(df7g)
x2g = df7g.x2
x3g = df7g.x3
eg  = df7g.e
ug  = df7g.u
yg  = df7g.y
DataFrames.describe(df7g)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Quantile regression for q = .25, .50 and .75
sqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.25 .50 .75)
=#
stata_summarize(df7g, [:e, :x2, :x3, :u, :y])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Predicted coefficient of x2 for q = .25, .50 and .75
quietly summarize e, detail
display "Predicted coefficient of x2 for q = .25, .50, and .75" ///
 _newline 1+.5*r(p25) _newline 1+.5*r(p50) _newline 1+.5*r(p75)
=#
# QR at τ = .25, .50, .75
Xg = hcat(ones(N7), df7g.x2, df7g.x3)
yg_v = df7g.y
qr25g = quantreg(Xg, yg_v, 0.25)
qr50g = quantreg(Xg, yg_v, 0.50)
qr75g = quantreg(Xg, yg_v, 0.75)
println("\n— QR at three quantiles —")
@printf("%-7s %10s %10s %10s\n", "Var", "QR25", "QR50", "QR75")
for (i, n) in enumerate(("_cons","x2","x3"))
    @printf("%-7s %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f\n", n, qr25g[i], qr50g[i], qr75g[i])
end
```


```{julia}
#=
* Generate scatterplots and qplot (replaced with custom quantile plot)
quietly kdensity u, scale(1.25) lwidth(medthick) saving(musr/density, replace)
* Create quantile plot for y
preserve
sort y
generate pctile_y = _n / _N
quietly twoway line y pctile_y, ///
    ytitle("y") xtitle("Fraction of the data") ///
    scale(1.4) lwidth(medthick) name(quanty, replace)
quietly graph save musr/quanty.gph, replace
restore
quietly scatter y x2, scale(1.25) saving(musr/yversusx2, replace)
quietly scatter y x3, scale(1.25) saving(musr/yversusx3, replace)
graph combine musr/density.gph musr/quanty.gph musr/yversusx2.gph musr/yversusx3.gph
quietly graph export musr/mus07hetexample.eps, replace
=#
#* Quantile regression for q = .25, .50 and .75
#sqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.25 .50 .75)
sq_fit7g = stata_sqreg(df7g, :y, [:x2, :x3];
                       τs=[0.25, 0.50, 0.75], reps=400, seed=10101);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Quantile regression for q = .25, .50 and .75
sqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.25 .50 .75)
=#
# Theoretical β_x2(τ) = 1 + 0.5·F_e^{-1}(τ) where e ~ N(0, 25)
σ_e = 5
for τ in (0.25, 0.50, 0.75)
    @printf("Predicted β_x2(τ=%.2f) = %.4f\n",
            τ, 1 + 0.5 * quantile(Normal(0, σ_e), τ))
end
```


```{julia}
#=
* Predicted coefficient of x2 for q = .25, .50 and .75
quietly summarize e, detail
display "Predicted coefficient of x2 for q = .25, .50, and .75" ///
 _newline 1+.5*r(p25) _newline 1+.5*r(p50) _newline 1+.5*r(p75)
=#
#* Predicted coefficient of x2 for q = .25, .50 and .75
#quietly summarize e, detail
#display "Predicted coefficient of x2 for q = .25, .50, and .75" ///
# _newline 1+.5*r(p25) _newline 1+.5*r(p50) #_newline 1+.5*r(p75)
println("Predicted coefficient of x2 for q = .25, .50, and .75")
for τ in (0.25, 0.50, 0.75)
    Printf.@printf("%.7g\n", 1 + 0.5 * Statistics.quantile(eg, τ))
end
```


```{julia}
# Density of u, quantile plot of y, and scatterplots
fig7g = Figure(size = (900, 700))
ax1 = Axis(fig7g[1, 1], title = "Density of u")
kdu = kde(df7g.y .- 1 .- df7g.x2 .- df7g.x3)
lines!(ax1, kdu.x, kdu.density)

ax2 = Axis(fig7g[1, 2], title = "Quantiles of y", xlabel = "fraction")
ys = sort(df7g.y); pct = (1:length(ys)) ./ length(ys)
lines!(ax2, pct, ys)

ax3 = Axis(fig7g[2, 1], title = "y vs x2", xlabel = "x2", ylabel = "y")
scatter!(ax3, df7g.x2, df7g.y, markersize = 1)

ax4 = Axis(fig7g[2, 2], title = "y vs x3", xlabel = "x3", ylabel = "y")
scatter!(ax4, df7g.x3, df7g.y, markersize = 1)
fig7g
```

```{julia}
#* Quantile regression for q = .25, .50 and .75
#sqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.25 .50 .75)
sq_fit7g = stata_sqreg(df7g, :y, [:x2, :x3];
                       τs=[0.25, 0.50, 0.75], reps=400, seed=10101);
```

### 7.4.2 QR estimates

```{julia}
#=
* OLS and quantile regression for q = .25, .5, .75
quietly regress y x2 x3
estimates store OLS
quietly regress y x2 x3, vce(robust)
estimates store OLS_Rob
quietly bsqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.25) reps(400)
estimates store QR_25
quietly bsqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.50) reps(400)
estimates store QR_50
quietly bsqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.75) reps(400)
estimates store QR_75
estimates table OLS OLS_Rob QR_25 QR_50 QR_75, b(%7.3f) se 
=#
# OLS and QR comparison on the simulated data
ols_g  = (Xg' * Xg) \ (Xg' * yg_v)
println("\n— OLS vs QR (simulated heteroskedastic data) —")
@printf("%-7s %10s %10s %10s %10s\n", "Var", "OLS", "QR25", "QR50", "QR75")
for (i, n) in enumerate(("_cons","x2","x3"))
    @printf("%-7s %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f\n",
            n, ols_g[i], qr25g[i], qr50g[i], qr75g[i])
end
```

```{julia}
#=
* Test equality of coeff of x2 for q=.25 and q=.75
set seed 10101
quietly sqreg y x2 x3, q(.25 .75) reps(400)
test [q25]x2 = [q75]x2
test [q25]x3 = [q75]x3
=#
#=
* OLS and quantile regression for q = .25, .5, .75
quietly regress y x2 x3
estimates store OLS
quietly regress y x2 x3, vce(robust)
estimates store OLS_Rob
quietly bsqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.25) reps(400)
estimates store QR_25
quietly bsqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.50) reps(400)
estimates store QR_50
quietly bsqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.75) reps(400)
estimates store QR_75
estimates table OLS OLS_Rob QR_25 QR_50 QR_75, b(%7.3f) se
=#
_wrap_fem(m) = (;
    β         = StatsBase.coef(m),
    V         = StatsBase.vcov(m),
    se        = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(StatsBase.vcov(m)), 0.0)),
    coefnames = replace.(string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m)), "(Intercept)" => "_cons"),
    n         = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m)),
)

OLS     = _wrap_fem(FixedEffectModels.reg(df7g, @formula(y ~ x2 + x3)))
OLS_Rob = _wrap_fem(FixedEffectModels.reg(df7g, @formula(y ~ x2 + x3),
                                          Vcov.robust()))

# Bootstrap bsqreg: separate resampling stream for each quantile.
function _bsqreg(df, y, xs, τ; reps::Int=400, seed::Int=10101)
    Random.seed!(seed)
    fit0 = stata_qreg(df, y, xs; τ=τ, quiet=true)
    nobs = DataFrames.nrow(df)
    β_b  = Matrix{Float64}(undef, reps, length(fit0.β))
    for b in 1:reps
        idx = StatsBase.sample(1:nobs, nobs; replace=true)
        β_b[b, :] = stata_qreg(df[idx, :], y, xs; τ=τ, quiet=true).β
    end
    return (; β = fit0.β,
              se = vec(Statistics.std(β_b; dims=1)),
              coefnames = fit0.coefnames,
              n = fit0.n)
end

QR_25 = _bsqreg(df7g, :y, [:x2, :x3], 0.25; reps=400, seed=10101)
QR_50 = _bsqreg(df7g, :y, [:x2, :x3], 0.50; reps=400, seed=10101)
QR_75 = _bsqreg(df7g, :y, [:x2, :x3], 0.75; reps=400, seed=10101)

stata_estimates_table(
    [OLS, OLS_Rob, QR_25, QR_50, QR_75],
    ["OLS", "OLS_Rob", "QR_25", "QR_50", "QR_75"];
    vars_order = ["x2", "x3", "_cons"],
    fmt        = "%7.3f")
```

```{julia}
# Bootstrap test that β_x2(τ=0.25) ≠ β_x2(τ=0.75)
Random.seed!(10101)
diffs = Float64[]
for _ in 1:200
    idx = sample(1:N7, N7; replace = true)
    b25 = quantreg(Xg[idx, :], yg_v[idx], 0.25)
    b75 = quantreg(Xg[idx, :], yg_v[idx], 0.75)
    push!(diffs, b25[2] - b75[2])
end
@printf("β_x2(.25) - β_x2(.75) = %.4f  bs.se = %.4f  z = %.2f\n",
        mean(diffs), std(diffs), mean(diffs) / std(diffs))
```

```{julia}
#* Test equality of coeff of x2 for q=.25 and q=.75
#set seed 10101
#quietly sqreg y x2 x3, q(.25 .75) reps(400)
#test [q25]x2 = [q75]x2
#test [q25]x3 = [q75]x3
sq_25_75 = stata_sqreg(df7g, :y, [:x2, :x3];
                       τs=[0.25, 0.75], reps=400, seed=10101);

# test [q25]x2 = [q75]x2
stata_test_sureg(sq_25_75, ["[q25]x2 = [q75]x2"]; df_F = sq_25_75.df_resid);

# test [q25]x3 = [q75]x3
stata_test_sureg(sq_25_75, ["[q25]x3 = [q75]x3"]; df_F = sq_25_75.df_resid);
```

## 7.5 QR for count data

### 7.5.1 Quantile count regression

### 7.5.2 The qcount approach

### 7.5.3 Summary of doctor visits data

```{julia}
#=
* Read in doctor visits count data and summarize
use musr/mus07qrcnt.dta, clear
summarize docvis private totchr age female white, separator(0)
=#
df7q = DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus07qrcnt.dta"))
for c in names(df7q)
    if eltype(df7q[!, c]) <: Union{Missing, Float32} || eltype(df7q[!, c]) === Float32
        df7q[!, c] = convert.(Union{Missing, Float64}, df7q[!, c])
    end
end
DataFrames.describe(df7q[:, [:docvis, :private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white]])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Generate jittered values and compare quantile plots
set seed 10101
generate docvisu = docvis + runiform()
* Create quantile plot for docvis
preserve
keep if docvis < 40
sort docvis
generate pctile_dv = _n / _N
quietly twoway line docvis pctile_dv, ///
    ytitle("docvis") xtitle("Fraction of the data") ///
    scale(1.25) lwidth(medthick) name(docvisqplot, replace)
quietly graph save musr/docvisqplot.gph, replace
restore
* Create quantile plot for docvisu
preserve
keep if docvis < 40
sort docvisu
generate pctile_dvu = _n / _N
quietly twoway line docvisu pctile_dvu, ///
    ytitle("docvisu") xtitle("Fraction of the data") ///
    scale(1.25) lwidth(medthick) name(docvisuqplot, replace)
quietly graph save musr/docvisuqplot.gph, replace
restore
graph combine musr/docvisqplot.gph musr/docvisuqplot.gph
graph export musr/mus07dvqplot2.eps, replace
=#
stata_summarize(df7q, [:docvis, :private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Marginal effects from conventional negative binomial model
quietly nbreg docvis i.private totchr age i.female i.white, vce(robust)
margins, dydx(*) atmean noatlegend
=#
# Quantile plots: docvis vs jittered docvisu
Random.seed!(10101)
df7q.docvisu = df7q.docvis .+ rand(nrow(df7q))
sub = filter(r -> r.docvis < 40, df7q)

fig7q = Figure(size = (900, 400))
ax1 = Axis(fig7q[1, 1], xlabel = "fraction of data", ylabel = "docvis")
ys = sort(sub.docvis); pct = (1:length(ys)) ./ length(ys)
lines!(ax1, pct, ys)

ax2 = Axis(fig7q[1, 2], xlabel = "fraction of data", ylabel = "docvisu (jittered)")
ys2 = sort(sub.docvisu); pct2 = (1:length(ys2)) ./ length(ys2)
lines!(ax2, pct2, ys2)
fig7q
```

```{julia}
# Negative binomial model and average marginal effects
nb7 = negbin(@formula(docvis ~ private + totchr + age + female + white),
             df7q, LogLink())
println(nb7)

# AME for Poisson/NB: ∂μ/∂x_j = β_j · μ(x); average over sample
df7q_clean = dropmissing(df7q, [:docvis, :private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white])
μhat = GLM.predict(nb7, df7q_clean)
println("\n— Average marginal effects (NB) —")
for (name, β) in zip(StatsBase.coefnames(nb7), StatsBase.coef(nb7))
    name == "(Intercept)" && continue
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", name, β * mean(μhat))
end
```

```{julia}
#* Marginal effects from conventional negative binomial model
#quietly nbreg docvis i.private totchr age i.female i.white, vce(robust)
#margins, dydx(*) atmean noatlegend
df7m = dropmissing(df7q, [:docvis, :private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white])
nb7m = GLM.negbin(@formula(docvis ~ private + totchr + age + female + white),
                  df7m, GLM.LogLink())
stata_margins_count(nb7m, df7m;
              binary = [:private, :female, :white],
              atmean = true);
```

```{julia}
#* Marginal effects from conventional negative binomial model
#quietly nbreg docvis i.private totchr age i.female i.white, vce(robust)
#margins, dydx(*) atmean noatlegend
#
# MEM for NB model: for continuous x_j, dy/dx_j = β_j · μ(x̄);
# for binary (i.) regressors use the discrete change μ(x=1) − μ(x=0).
# SEs by the delta method with the nbreg vcov.
df7m = dropmissing(df7q, [:docvis, :private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white])
nb7m = GLM.negbin(@formula(docvis ~ private + totchr + age + female + white),
                  df7m, GLM.LogLink())
stata_margins_count(nb7m, df7m;
              binary = [:private, :female, :white],
              atmean = true);
```

### 7.5.4 Results from quantile count regression

```{julia}
# Quantile count regression (Machado–Santos Silva 2005) at τ = 0.50
# Algorithm: (1) jitter the count y → y* = y + U(0,1)
#            (2) run standard QR on log(y*) at the desired τ
#            (3) repeat for R replications, average the coefficients
#            (4) marginal effects: dy/dx = exp(x'β) · β_j  (for continuous x)
Random.seed!(10101)
df7qc = dropmissing(df7q, [:docvis, :private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white])
yq = Float64.(df7qc.docvis)
Xq = hcat(ones(nrow(df7qc)), df7qc.private, df7qc.totchr, df7qc.age,
          df7qc.female, df7qc.white)
xnms_q = ["_cons", "private", "totchr", "age", "female", "white"]
nq = length(yq)

function qcount_est(yq, Xq, τ; R = 500, seed = 10101)
    rng = Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
    p = size(Xq, 2)
    β_sum = zeros(p)
    for r in 1:R
        ystar = yq .+ rand(rng, nq)
        ly    = log.(ystar)
        β_sum .+= quantreg(Xq, ly, τ)
    end
    return β_sum ./ R
end

β_qcr50 = qcount_est(yq, Xq, 0.50; R = 500)
println("— Quantile count regression at τ = 0.50 —")
for i in eachindex(β_qcr50)
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", xnms_q[i], β_qcr50[i])
end
```

```{julia}
#=
* Quantile count regression
* Install qcount if not already installed
capture which qcount
if _rc != 0 {
    ssc install qcount
}
set seed 10101
qcount docvis private totchr age female white, q(0.50) rep(500)
=#
qc_fit = stata_qcount(df7q, :docvis,
                      [:private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white];
                      τ=0.50, reps=500, seed=10101);
```


```{julia}
# qcount_mfx at τ = 0.50  — marginal effects: ME_j = β_j · exp(x̄'β)
xbar = vec(mean(Xq, dims = 1))
expxb = exp(dot(xbar, β_qcr50))
println("— QCR marginal effects at τ = 0.50 (at sample means) —")
for i in 2:length(β_qcr50)   # skip constant
    @printf("%-10s  dy/dx = %12.6f\n", xnms_q[i], β_qcr50[i] * expxb)
end
```

```{julia}
#=
* Marginal effects after quantile regression for median
set linesize 81
qcount_mfx
=#
stata_margins_count(qc_fit, df7q;
              binary = [:private, :female, :white],
              atmean = true);

```

```{julia}
# Quantile count regression at τ = 0.75 + marginal effects
β_qcr75 = qcount_est(yq, Xq, 0.75; R = 500)
expxb75 = exp(dot(xbar, β_qcr75))
println("— Quantile count regression at τ = 0.75 —")
for i in eachindex(β_qcr75)
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", xnms_q[i], β_qcr75[i])
end
println("\n— QCR marginal effects at τ = 0.75 (at sample means) —")
for i in 2:length(β_qcr75)
    @printf("%-10s  dy/dx = %12.6f\n", xnms_q[i], β_qcr75[i] * expxb75)
end
```


```{julia}
#=
* Quantile count regression for q = 0.75
set seed 10101
quietly qcount docvis private totchr age female white, q(0.75) rep(500)
qcount_mfx
=#
qc_fit75 = stata_qcount(df7q, :docvis,
                      [:private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white];
                      τ=0.75, reps=500, seed=10101);
stata_margins_count(qc_fit75, df7q;
              binary = [:private, :female, :white],
              atmean = true);
```

## 7.6 Stata resources

## 7.7 Exercises

```{julia}
#=
* Save coefficients and generate graph for a range of quantiles
use musr/mus03data.dta, clear
drop if ltotexp == . 
capture program drop mus07plot
program mus07plot
  postfile myfile percentile b1 upper lower using musr/bsqrcoef1.dta, replace 
  forvalues tau1=0.10(0.1)0.9 {
  set seed 10101
  quietly bsqreg ltotexp suppins age female white totchr, quant(`tau1') reps(400)
  matrix b = e(b)
  scalar b1=b[1,1]
  matrix V = e(V)
  scalar v1=V[1,1]
  scalar df=e(df_r) 
  scalar upper = b1 + invttail(df,.025)*sqrt(v1)
  scalar lower = b1 - invttail(df,.025)*sqrt(v1)
  post myfile (`tau1') (b1) (upper) (lower) 
  matrix drop V b
  scalar drop b1 v1 upper lower df
  }
  postclose myfile
end
mus07plot
program drop mus07plot
use musr/bsqrcoef1.dta, clear
twoway connected b1 percentile || line upper percentile||line lower percentile, ///
  title("Slope Estimates") subtitle("Coefficient of suppins") ///
  xtitle("Quantile", size(medlarge)) ///
  ytitle("Slope and confidence bands", size(medlarge)) ///
  legend(label(1 "Quantile slope coefficient") ///
  label(2 "Upper 95% bs confidence band")  ///
  label(3 "Lower 95% bs confidence band"))  
graph save musr/bsqrcoef1.gph, replace
=#
# Save quantile-coefficient series for τ = 0.10, 0.20, ..., 0.90 and plot
df7p = dropmissing(DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus03data.dta"))[:,
        [:ltotexp, :suppins, :age, :female, :white, :totchr]])
yp = df7p.ltotexp
Xp = hcat(ones(nrow(df7p)),
          df7p.suppins, df7p.age, df7p.female, df7p.white, df7p.totchr)

τs = 0.10:0.10:0.90
b1s = Float64[]; ups = Float64[]; los = Float64[]
Random.seed!(10101)
for τ in τs
    β = quantreg(Xp, yp, τ)
    push!(b1s, β[2])  # suppins
    bs = Float64[]
    for _ in 1:50
        idx = sample(1:length(yp), length(yp); replace = true)
        push!(bs, quantreg(Xp[idx, :], yp[idx], τ)[2])
    end
    se = std(bs)
    push!(ups, β[2] + 1.96 * se)
    push!(los, β[2] - 1.96 * se)
end

fig_qr = Figure(size = (700, 450))
ax = Axis(fig_qr[1, 1],
          title = "Slope of suppins across quantiles",
          xlabel = "Quantile", ylabel = "Coefficient and 95% CI")
band!(ax, collect(τs), los, ups, color = (:steelblue, 0.3))
lines!(ax, collect(τs), b1s, linewidth = 2)
scatter!(ax, collect(τs), b1s)
fig_qr
```
